characters.physical_details: 6'1", lean sprinter's build with engineered bilateral symmetry that looks slightly too perfect. Synthetic muscle grafts in both legs (invisible under skin but he can feel the seams at the quadriceps and calf interface). Spinal neural bridge — no visible external hardware. Visual processing enhancement. Post-Chapter 5: facial lacerations (left cheek, brow), broken left wrist (heals abnormally fast due to graft-accelerated bone repair), dislocated right shoulder. The wrist develops a persistent ache where the bone knitted too quickly — a permanent detail.

key_relationships.lena_morozova: romantic partner, bonded through shared bodily alienation
key_relationships.yusuf_qadir: athlete-engineer relationship, trust broken and partially rebuilt
key_relationships.margot_voss: initial indifference, escalates to confrontation over her treatment of Lena
key_relationships.tomas_reyes: senior athlete looked up to by Tomas; Tomas's injury becomes Kai's moral weight

characters.literacy_languages: English (native). Basic Spanish from high school. No other languages.

state_transitions.change: Injured: dislocated right shoulder, broken left wrist, facial lacerations from blackout crash
Begins private override training — testing conscious control of synthetic muscle autonomous firing
Discovers Yusuf's archive; relationship with Yusuf fractures
Competes unpatched; virus triggers during race; finishes last under own power
In Lausanne post-Games; commentator offer pending; undecided

characters.availability: Present throughout. Chapters 1, 5, 7, 11, 15, 17 as primary POV. Medical bay recovery Ch 6-7 (mobile but injured).

characters.physical_details: 5'10", swimmer's build — broad shoulders, long torso. Neural link interface visible as a small circular port (2cm diameter) at the base of her skull, usually covered by her hair. No other visible enhancement hardware. When the desync is active, observers may notice a flattening of her facial expression and a slight change in her eye movement patterns.

key_relationships.margot_voss: coach-athlete relationship; trust betrayed in Ch 10
key_relationships.yusuf_qadir: engineer who installs her patch; professional trust
key_relationships.dara_lund: training partner; Dara represents a further-along version of Lena's condition

characters.occupation_status: Professional Apex Games swimmer (400m, 800m freestyle), Meridian developmental program athlete since birth
characters.literacy_languages: German (native), Russian (fluent, parents' language), English (fluent, slightly formal register), French (functional — Swiss schooling).

state_transitions.change: Desync becomes legible — motor control and cognitive symptoms
Desync severity revealed to be far worse than reported; loses segments of conscious experience
Receives Yusuf's partial patch — desync stabilizes but sensory acuity blunted, reaction times slower, silence where system processing was
Competes on the patch; places 3rd in 400m freestyle
Training at a smaller facility in Montreux, independent of Meridian's program

characters.availability: Present throughout. Chapters 2, 10, 11, 13, 17 as primary POV.

characters.physical_details: 5'9", slight build. Dark hair, kept short. Glasses (actual prescription, not enhanced — he has declined personal enhancement). Perpetually slightly rumpled. Hands are notably steady and precise — an engineer's hands.

key_relationships.kai_brennan: professional relationship fractured by archive discovery in Ch 14
key_relationships.lena_morozova: professional trust — he installs her patch
key_relationships.petra_engel: professional alliance built on shared institutional distrust
key_relationships.farah_qadir: younger sister with Voss-Kessler syndrome; his primary personal motivation
key_relationships.margot_voss: professional colleagues; he designs the patch that would sever her feed access

characters.occupation_status: Lead calibration engineer, Kirchberg Institute. PhD from Imperial College London in biomechanical systems.
characters.literacy_languages: English (native, Birmingham dialect), Urdu (fluent, family language), Arabic (reading proficiency). Technical literacy in multiple programming and firmware languages.

state_transitions.change: Finds Meridian signatures in virus code; begins building alternative patch
Reveals Farah's condition to the group; trust complicated rather than earned
Archive discovered by Kai; confrontation; reveals Meridian conspiracy suspicion
Publishes Meridian analysis; investigation triggered

characters.availability: Present throughout. Chapters 3, 9 as primary POV. Present in calibration scenes throughout.

characters.physical_details: 5'8", former swimmer's build carrying more weight than her competitive years. Self-conscious about this in the facility's environment of optimized bodies. Neural coaching feed interface is a wrist-mounted device that she connects to the athlete's system — it is personal equipment, not facility-issued.

key_relationships.lena_morozova: coach-athlete; trust betrayed by Margot's concealment
key_relationships.kai_brennan: confrontational after her treatment of Lena is revealed
key_relationships.tomas_reyes: coach-athlete; she pushes him dangerously; his paralysis is partly her consequence
key_relationships.petra_engel: Petra ultimately reviews Margot's feed logs and suspends her

characters.occupation_status: Neural performance coach at Kirchberg Institute. Former professional swimmer (5th place, 2028 Olympics).

state_transitions.change: Pushes Tomas too hard; the pattern of heart-rate elevation begins
Conceals Lena's desync severity to preserve feed access
Experiences Tomas's crash through the feed at full sensory resolution
Suspended from coaching; feed access revoked; addiction exposed
In neural addiction treatment facility in Munich

characters.availability: Present through Ch 16. Not present at Apex Games venue (Ch 17) — still at Kirchberg, grounded. Ch 18: in Munich treatment facility.

characters.physical_details: Tall, angular, grey-streaked dark hair usually pinned back. Carries herself with clinical precision. Looks tired in a way that suggests chronic rather than acute exhaustion.

key_relationships.yusuf_qadir: professional alliance; she covers for his unauthorized work
key_relationships.margot_voss: medical oversight; she discovers and acts on Margot's feed abuse

characters.occupation_status: Medical director, Kirchberg Institute. Previously at a Meridian facility in Stuttgart where an athlete died from enhancement rejection.

state_transitions.change: Discovers Yusuf's unauthorized access and covers for him
Reviews Margot's feed logs; suspends her from coaching

characters.availability: Present at Kirchberg throughout Chapters 1-16. Not present at Apex Games venue.

characters.physical_details: 5'11", decathlon build — muscular but balanced across multiple disciplines. Young face, expressive. Enhancement is relatively recent (3 years) — his body still has visible asymmetries that older enhanced athletes have had calibrated away.

key_relationships.kai_brennan: idolizes Kai as a senior athlete who made the jump from natural to enhanced

characters.occupation_status: Professional Apex Games decathlete. First Games. Meridian-sponsored. Family in Quito dependent on sponsorship income.
characters.literacy_languages: Spanish (native), English (fluent, some word-searching under stress).

state_transitions.change: Falls during hurdle training due to Margot's heart-rate push; minor injury
Signs Meridian data contract immediately — cannot afford to refuse
Virus-triggered blackout during hurdles; spinal impact; paralyzed from waist down; neural bridge destroyed; career ended

characters.availability: Present Chapters 1-14 at Kirchberg. After Ch 14: hospitalized, then transferred to a spinal rehabilitation facility. Not present Chapters 15-18.

characters.physical_details: Compact, professionally dressed. Not enhanced. Carries her brother's death in the careful way she handles enhancement hardware — she touches it precisely, not casually.

key_relationships.lena_morozova: Lena invites her to the Ch 12 argument; professional respect
key_relationships.yusuf_qadir: opposes his alternative patch from genuine conviction

characters.occupation_status: Athlete liaison, Meridian BioSystems. Corporate representative but not a corporate cynic — driven by her brother's death in 2034.

state_transitions.change: Arrives at Kirchberg with Meridian delegation; presents the data contract
Argues against Yusuf's alternative from position of genuine conviction

characters.availability: Present from Chapter 8 onward at Kirchberg. Arrives with corporate delegation.

characters.physical_details: Swimmer's build, Nordic coloring. Looks healthy, moves efficiently. The uncanny quality is subtle — a flatness in conversational affect, a tendency to respond to emotional questions with functional answers. She does not appear to be suffering.

key_relationships.lena_morozova: training partner; swimming same events; represents Lena's possible future

characters.occupation_status: Professional Apex Games swimmer (400m, 800m freestyle). Bergen Performance Institute program.
characters.availability: Present at Kirchberg throughout. Background presence. Visible in Ch 2 (training partner) and Ch 13 (reacts to Lena's patch).

characters.physical_details: Not physically present in the story. Seen only via video call. Cheerful face, dark hair. Her Voss-Kessler symptoms are visible as slightly slowed speech and occasional word-finding difficulty.

key_relationships.yusuf_qadir: older brother; his primary personal motivation

characters.occupation_status: Lives in Birmingham. Employed status unclear — the degenerative condition is progressive.

state_transitions.change: Speech slightly slower than last month during video call — progression visible

characters.availability: Never physically present. Appears via video call in Ch 3. Referenced in Ch 12 and Ch 18.

timeline.story_start: Late January 2038 — winter in the Bernese Alps, six months before the Apex Games opening ceremony
timeline.estimated_span: Approximately seven months: late January through mid-August 2038

seasonal_track.season: Late winter — deep snow in the Alps, short days, facility operating under artificial lighting much of the day. Temperature: -5 to -15°C outside, controlled 21°C inside.
Late winter into early spring — snowmelt beginning at lower elevations, days lengthening. The facility's outdoor areas become accessible. Temperature moderating.
Spring — thaw in progress, mud and runoff, longer days, the mountains transitioning.
Late spring/early summer — compressed time covering several weeks. Departure for Doha.
Late July — Apex Games in Doha, extreme heat (40°C+), air-conditioned venues.
August — summer in Switzerland (Lausanne, Montreux), summer in Munich. Warm, long days.

key_events.event: First sensory blackout — Kai's crash on the indoor track
Meridian delegation arrives with patch offer
key_events.timing: Approximately 1 week after blackout (3 weeks after arrival)

geography.primary_setting: The Kirchberg Institute — a high-altitude training and calibration facility in the Bernese Alps, Switzerland. Elevation approximately 2,100 meters. Accessible by a single road from the valley town of Meiringen. The facility is a purpose-built campus: training halls, calibration bays, medical wing, residential quarters, cafeteria, and administrative offices. Architecture is concrete and glass — functional, well-equipped, institutional.

key_locations.name: Calibration Bay
key_locations.details: Sterile, temperature-controlled room with calibration rigs — reclining chairs connected to diagnostic and firmware systems. Fluorescent lighting. The hum of processing equipment is constant. This is where athletes lie still while their systems are tuned. Yusuf's primary workspace.

key_locations.name: Indoor Track
key_locations.details: 200m oval, altitude-controlled. Rubber surface, high ceilings with acoustic dampening. The echo of footfalls carries. The curve where Kai crashes in Ch 5 has a concrete wall with padding that was not designed for sprint-speed impact.

key_locations.name: Olympic Pool
key_locations.details: 50m, 10 lanes, temperature-controlled (27°C water). Chlorine smell is constant. Underwater acoustics — the muffled quality of sound below the surface. Observation deck with glass wall for coaching staff and feed monitoring.

key_locations.name: Medical Bay
key_locations.details: Full clinical facility — imaging, surgical suite, recovery rooms. Operated by Dr. Petra Engel's team. Located adjacent to the calibration bay for convenience.

key_locations.name: Cafeteria
key_locations.details: Central communal space. Long tables, institutional food with high-protein options. Windows facing the mountain ridge. Where the four principals eat the meal in Ch 16.

key_locations.name: Athletes' Quarters
key_locations.details: Individual rooms in a residential wing. Spartan but equipped with personal monitoring systems. Each room has a bed, desk, bathroom, and a wall-mounted display showing real-time enhancement diagnostics.

key_locations.name: Neural Coaching Suite
key_locations.details: Smaller room adjacent to training areas where Margot connects to the feed interface. The coaching rig has two stations — one for the coach, one for the athlete. Monitoring displays on the wall.

key_locations.name: Doha Apex Games Venue
key_locations.details: Purpose-built sports complex in Doha, Qatar. Extreme heat outside, air-conditioned inside. The Apex Games venue is architecturally spectacular — glass and steel, designed for global broadcast. Ch 17 only.

distances: Kirchberg Institute to Meiringen (nearest town): approximately 30 minutes by road, single mountain road
Kirchberg Institute to Zurich: approximately 2 hours by road
Kirchberg Institute to Doha Apex Games venue: international flight, roughly 6 hours
Calibration Bay to Indoor Track: same building, adjacent wings, approximately 3 minutes' walk through a connecting corridor
Athletes' Quarters to Cafeteria: same campus, approximately 5 minutes' walk
Lausanne to Montreux: approximately 30 minutes by train along Lake Geneva

world_rules: The Apex Games operate under separate governance from the Traditional Olympic Games. Both competitions occur in the same year but at different venues.

Bio-integrative enhancement is legal and regulated within the Apex Games framework. Types include: neural links (brain-computer interfaces for sensory and motor augmentation), synthetic muscle grafts (artificial muscle tissue integrated with natural muscle), engineered bone density, sensory augmentation (enhanced vision, hearing, proprioception), and spinal neural bridges (artificial nerve pathways replacing or supplementing natural spinal cord function).

Enhancement hardware requires periodic calibration through a shared network infrastructure. Athletes at the same facility share a calibration network — their systems communicate during tuning to establish optimal parameters. This shared network is the virus's propagation vector.

Neural coaching feeds allow a coach to experience an athlete's sensory input in real time through a dedicated interface. The feed is one-directional (coach receives, does not transmit sensation to athlete) but the connection opens a bidirectional data pathway that may serve as a virus vector.

Enhancement systems have autonomous subsystems that can operate without conscious input — synthetic muscle can fire to prevent injury, neural links can optimize motor patterns without conscious selection, visual processing can pre-filter sensory data. These autonomous functions are normally invisible to the user but become noticeable when they malfunction or when the user becomes aware of the gap between intent and execution.

Voss-Kessler syndrome is a progressive neural deterioration condition. It is rare, poorly understood, and currently untreatable. It causes gradual loss of neural function — beginning with fine motor control and speech processing, eventually affecting cognition. It is not related to enhancement technology.

The virus propagates through the shared calibration network. It corrupts the firmware that governs sensory processing, causing violent sensory blackouts — total loss of all sensory input for periods of 10-60 seconds. During a blackout, autonomous subsystems may continue operating, driving the body without conscious control.

Meridian BioSystems is the dominant corporate entity in the Apex Games enhancement ecosystem. They manufacture and maintain most enhancement hardware, sponsor athletes, operate calibration networks, and fund training facilities including the Kirchberg Institute. Their proposed patch requires permanent data ownership as a contractual condition.

The data surrender contract ('irrevocable, transferable license') gives Meridian permanent ownership of all biometric, genetic, neural, and physiological data generated by or through the Enhancement System. This is not limited to enhancement data — it includes baseline biological information. The license is transferable, meaning Meridian can sell or sublicense the data.

power_structure.mechanism: Contractual sponsorship, proprietary enhancement hardware, control of calibration network infrastructure, and now the data surrender contract. Athletes depend on Meridian for equipment maintenance and continued system function.

power_structure.over: Athletes and staff at the facility
power_structure.mechanism: Institutional authority over training schedules, facility access, and operational decisions. But the institute is Meridian-funded, limiting its independence.
Medical director authority — she can ground athletes, order examinations, suspend staff (as she does with Margot). Her authority is real but constrained by Meridian's institutional influence.

Technical access to calibration and firmware. Athletes must trust their engineer with their most intimate systems. Yusuf's breach of this trust (the unauthorized data collection) is significant because of how much access his role grants.
The neural feed gives her direct access to an athlete's sensory experience and indirect influence over their performance parameters. The power is intimate and largely unmonitored.

objects.name: Yusuf's encrypted personal archive
objects.origin: Personal device brought to the facility
objects.status: Active and growing through Ch 14, then discovered by Kai. Data eventually published in Ch 18.

objects.name: Margot's feed interface
objects.origin: Personal coaching equipment, not facility-issued. Wrist-mounted device that connects to the athlete's neural system.
objects.status: Active through Ch 15, then access revoked by Petra.

objects.name: Meridian data surrender contract
objects.origin: Produced by Meridian legal team in response to the virus
objects.status: Presented Ch 8. Most athletes sign. Kai, Lena, and a few holdouts refuse.

objects.name: Kai's wrist brace
objects.status: Worn through Ch 7-8 as the wrist heals. The wrist aches persistently through the end of the novel — it healed too fast and not quite right.

objects.name: Yusuf's partial patch
objects.origin: Developed by Yusuf in the calibration bay, Ch 9-12
objects.status: Installed in Lena Ch 13. Other holdout athletes receive it during compressed time of Ch 16. Kai refuses it.

income_sources.amount: Variable by athlete tier. Tomas's contract includes family support provisions that make refusal economically devastating.
income_sources.reliability: Dependent on continued Apex Games participation and compliance with Meridian's terms. The data contract adds a new compliance layer.

income_sources.amount: Not specified, but the facility's operations depend on it.
income_sources.reliability: Institutional — the facility cannot operate independently of Meridian's support, which constrains the administration's willingness to challenge Meridian's decisions.

knowledge_state.knows: The virus may be deliberately engineered by Meridian — structural signatures in the code match Meridian's development tools
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Everyone until chapter_14 when he tells Kai

knowledge_state.knows: He is collecting unauthorized biological data from athletes for his sister's treatment research
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Everyone except Petra suspects. Kai discovers in chapter_14. Group learns in chapter_12 (partial — he reveals Farah's condition but not the data collection scope).

knowledge_state.knows: Lena's desync is far more severe than reported — she loses entire segments of conscious experience
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Lena (who suspects but cannot confirm), Kai, Yusuf, Petra — until chapter_15 when the feed logs are reviewed

knowledge_state.knows: The neural coaching feed may be a virus transmission vector
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Everyone until chapter_15 when feed log review exposes the connection pattern

knowledge_state.knows: She systematically pushes athletes' heart rates past safe margins during feed sessions for personal gratification
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Everyone until chapter_15 when Petra reviews the telemetry data

knowledge_state.knows: Yusuf is conducting unauthorized after-hours work in the calibration bay
knowledge_state.hidden_from: She covers for him — hidden from facility administration and Meridian

knowledge_state.knows: Her brother died from enhancement cascade failure in 2034
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Not hidden — she shares it publicly in chapter_08. But its influence on her institutional advocacy is something she does not fully examine.

knowledge_state.knows: His proprioception scores show system-initiated micro-movements ahead of conscious intent
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Not actively hidden, but Kai does not discuss it openly until chapter_11 with Lena

environmental_constants: The Kirchberg Institute is at 2,100m elevation — thin air affects breathing, recovery, and exertion. Characters who have been at the facility for weeks are acclimatized; new arrivals notice the altitude.
The calibration bay hums constantly — a low-frequency sound from the processing equipment that staff stop noticing but visitors remark on.
The pool smells of chlorine. Lena's hair always carries a faint chlorine trace.
The indoor track has a specific rubber smell and the echo of footfalls carries in the high-ceilinged space.

Mountain weather is visible through the facility's glass walls — snow in early chapters, gradual thaw through the middle, full spring by the departure chapters. The mountains are a persistent visual presence.
The athletes' quarters have wall-mounted diagnostic displays that show real-time enhancement status. These glow faintly at night.
The neural link interface port at the base of Lena's skull is a tactile detail — she touches it unconsciously, the way someone might touch a scar.
Kai's wrist aches persistently from Chapter 7 onward — the bone healed too fast and not quite right. This is a recurring physical detail.
The facility's lighting is predominantly artificial — fluorescent in clinical spaces, warmer in residential areas. Natural light comes through large windows but is limited in winter.
Doha in Ch 17 is the thermal inverse of the Alps: extreme heat, humidity, the shock of stepping from air-conditioning into 40°C air.
